title: How to auto-number rows with a formula
task: Row numbers that renumber themselves when rows are added, deleted, or sorted.

excel.note: Excel 365: one formula spills numbers for every data row. Universal per-row: =ROW()-1.

explanation: SEQUENCE(rows, cols, start, step) generates a number series in one spill — the verified example makes 10, 20, 30 (3 rows starting at 10, stepping 10). For row numbering, SEQUENCE(COUNTA(B2:B100)) sizes itself to the data. The old-school =ROW()-1 filled down also survives inserts/deletes (each cell recomputes its own position) but breaks under sorting — typed literal numbers break under everything, which is why formulas beat typing 1,2,3.
